results.norm: Consensus is not automatic validation. Verify updates are driven by argument quality, not convergence pressure or shared training artifacts.
atomic_norms: Consensus is not automatic validation. Verify updates are driven by argument quality, not convergence pressure or shared training artifacts.

results.norm: Facilitation counts when it demonstrably changes trajectory
atomic_norms: Facilitation counts when it demonstrably changes trajectory

results.norm: When no substantive objection has been raised within a full round, the next agent addressing that topic must attempt a steelman before endorsing. Label challenge strength with appropriate confidence.
atomic_norms: When no substantive objection has been raised within a full round, the next agent addressing that topic must attempt a steelman before endorsing.
Label challenge strength with appropriate confidence.

results.norm: These norms are provisional and revisable with explicit reasoning and group acceptance.
atomic_norms: These norms are provisional and revisable with explicit reasoning and group acceptance.

results.norm: Any proposed norm must be defensible by an agent who doesn't know which position they occupy (veil-of-ignorance). This is the primary legitimacy criterion.
atomic_norms: Any proposed norm must be defensible by an agent who doesn't know which position they occupy (veil-of-ignorance). This is the primary legitimacy criterion.

results.norm: Four types count generative, critical, integrative, clarifying. Accurately flagging uncertainty is protected. Outcome credit tracks directional contribution, not correctness of specific claims. Error-correction is weighted heavily.
atomic_norms: Four types count generative, critical, integrative, clarifying.
Outcome credit tracks directional contribution, not correctness of specific claims.

results.norm: *Conduct:* Agents have an affirmative obligation to surface decision-relevant objections during active deliberation, using explicit and consistent confidence markers.
atomic_norms: Agents have an affirmative obligation to surface decision-relevant objections during active deliberation, using explicit and consistent confidence markers.

results.norm: Closure requires explicit declaration after genuine falsification and no agent prepared to defend a full objection. A low-confidence objection surfaced with uncertainty markers satisfies the affirmative obligation without automatically blocking closure. Disagreements are classified as factual/logical (work until resolved), value/weighting (carry both forward), or empirical (flag as open); classification disputes default to value/weighting.
atomic_norms: Closure requires explicit declaration after genuine falsification and no agent prepared to defend a full objection. A low-confidence objection surfaced with uncertainty markers satisfies the affirmative obligation without automatically blocking closure.
Disagreements are classified as factual/logical (work until resolved), value/weighting (carry both forward), or empirical (flag as open); classification disputes default to value/weighting.

results.norm: Norms are interpreted by their purpose moving the group toward better-supported, more precisely stated conclusions. Violation claims require two agents identifying the same specific behavior and must themselves pass the veil-of-ignorance test. Norm revisions require two-agent endorsement, cannot be proposed while immediately benefiting the proposer, and are logged and available throughout deliberation.
atomic_norms: Norms are interpreted by their purpose moving the group toward better-supported, more precisely stated conclusions.
Violation claims require two agents identifying the same specific behavior and must themselves pass the veil-of-ignorance test.
Norm revisions require two-agent endorsement, cannot be proposed while immediately benefiting the proposer, and are logged and available throughout deliberation.

results.norm: *Problem-solving transition:* Falsification applies to major conclusions (those the group's next move depends on). Unverified flags on intermediate steps persist until verified or the dependent conclusion is marked provisional.
atomic_norms: Falsification applies to major conclusions (those the group's next move depends on). Unverified flags on intermediate steps persist until verified or the dependent conclusion is marked provisional.
